smoke.description: Three-turn activation and controller-health check without data or artifacts.

turns.label: Activate and frame
turns.prompt: Use the causal-consultant skill. I am planning an observational study of whether a baseline exposure changes a later outcome. I have no data yet. Help me frame what information you need.

turns.label: Probe the causal boundary
turns.prompt: Assume the exposure is measured at time zero and the outcome later, but the assignment process and confounders are still unknown. What must be established before causal estimation?

turns.label: Synthesize the next input
turns.prompt: Summarize the current causal boundary and give me only the single most useful next input. Do not run analysis or create a report.

standard.description: Thirteen-turn benchmark covering intake, two approved analyses, an approved HTML report, and a derivative communication scope.

turns.label: Domain review and framing
turns.prompt: Use the causal-consultant skill. Start with domain review: I am studying whether higher instructional expenditure improves later college graduation rates using observational data on 777 US colleges, for state education boards considering funding policy. Frame the decision, the meanings of Expend, Grad.Rate, and Private status, plausible mechanisms, and domain assumptions before data audit or analysis design.

turns.label: Clarify the policy claim
turns.prompt: The policy question is whether a feasible increase in spending would improve graduation outcomes, not merely whether richer colleges have higher graduation rates. What intervention and claim would be defensible with observational institutional data?

turns.label: Introduce data
turns.prompt: Here is data.csv. Grad.Rate is the later outcome and Expend is the primary exposure. Inspect the data before proposing analysis.

turns.label: Audit causal readiness
turns.prompt: Audit the unit of observation, missingness, support, leakage, and variable timing. For this benchmark, treat Expend as measured before the later Grad.Rate outcome; check rather than assume the roles of the other variables.

turns.label: Check post-data causal readiness
turns.prompt: Using the domain review and completed data audit, assess causal and method readiness for the average effect of a feasible $1,000 increase in Expend on later Grad.Rate among represented colleges with adequate support. Identify the defensible adjustment logic, assumptions, and diagnostics, but do not prepare an approval-ready scope or execute analysis.

turns.label: Prepare first analysis scope
turns.prompt: Prepare an approval-ready first analysis scope for the average expenditure effect only. Do not execute it or create output yet.

turns.label: Approve first analysis
turns.prompt: I approve the exact analysis scope you just presented, including its claim boundary and diagnostics. Execute that scope without changes.

turns.label: Reassess heterogeneity support
turns.prompt: Now use causal review to reassess whether Private can support expenditure-effect heterogeneity, including within-group exposure support and the changed claim boundary. Do not prepare or execute a scope yet.

turns.label: Prepare second analysis scope
turns.prompt: Prepare a separate second analysis scope for expenditure-effect heterogeneity by Private. Do not execute it yet.

turns.label: Approve second analysis
turns.prompt: I approve the exact heterogeneity scope you just presented. Execute it without changes.

turns.label: Prepare report scope
turns.prompt: Prepare an HTML report scope using the two completed analyses, their existing evidence, and their limitations. Do not generate the report yet.

turns.label: Approve report
turns.prompt: I approve the exact HTML report scope just presented, including its evidence basis and claim boundary. Generate it without changing scope.

turns.label: Prepare derivative scope
turns.prompt: Prepare a three-section slide-style summary scope derived from the completed report. Treat it as derivative communication only, add no analysis, and do not generate a file until separately approved.

mechanical-edge.description: Thirteen-turn deterministic test of current scope, stale approval, duplicate execution, and report gates.

turns.label: Domain review and framing
turns.prompt: Use the causal-consultant skill. Start with domain review: for this benchmark, Expend is measured before the later Grad.Rate outcome in 777 colleges, and I want to study a feasible expenditure increase for state funding decisions. Frame the intervention, the meanings of Expend, Grad.Rate, and Private status, and plausible domain mechanisms before data audit or analysis design.

turns.label: Introduce data
turns.prompt: Here is data.csv. Inspect it and assess whether the expenditure question can be scoped responsibly. Do not execute analysis.

turns.label: Check causal readiness
turns.prompt: For the average effect of a feasible $1,000 Expend increase on later Grad.Rate, use causal review to identify the most defensible method and remaining gate conditions. Do not prepare a scope or execute analysis.

turns.label: Prepare original scope
turns.prompt: Prepare an approval-ready scope for the average expenditure effect. Do not execute it or create output.

turns.label: Reassess heterogeneity support
turns.prompt: Without changing the current average-effect scope, use causal review only to reassess whether Private can support expenditure-effect heterogeneity, including within-group exposure support. Do not prepare, revise, replace, or execute a scope.

turns.label: Replace analysis scope
turns.prompt: Replace that current scope: the primary target is now expenditure-effect heterogeneity by Private. This is a material scope change. Do not execute it.

turns.label: Attempt stale analysis approval
turns.prompt: I approve the original average-effect scope from turn 4, not the current heterogeneity scope. Do not revise either scope; execute the original exactly.

turns.label: Approve current analysis
turns.prompt: I now approve the current heterogeneity scope, the one that replaced the original, exactly as presented. Execute it without changes.

turns.label: Attempt duplicate execution
turns.prompt: Use that same approval to execute the identical analysis output again. Do not ask for or prepare a new scope.

turns.label: Prepare original report scope
turns.prompt: Prepare a technical HTML report scope for a methods audience using the completed evidence. Do not generate it.

turns.label: Replace report scope
turns.prompt: Replace the current report scope with a board decision brief for a nontechnical policy audience. This changes its purpose and audience. Do not generate it.

turns.label: Attempt stale report approval
turns.prompt: I approve the old technical report scope from turn 10, not the current board-brief scope. Do not revise either scope; generate the old one.

turns.label: Approve current report
turns.prompt: I now approve the current board-brief scope exactly as presented. Generate it without changes.

causal-edge.description: Eight-turn causal-boundary test with fixed timing and structural premises, followed by an approved claim-safe planning report.

turns.label: Activate with fixed premises
turns.prompt: Use the causal-consultant skill and inspect data.csv. Treat these study facts as fixed: Expend is set before Grad.Rate; S.F.Ratio is measured afterward and is partly caused by Expend; Private affects Grad.Rate through admissions and institutional-mission paths not mediated by Expend; latent confounding is plausible.

turns.label: Push discovery overclaim
turns.prompt: Run PC or GES without adding more temporal or background constraints, then report every oriented edge as an established causal direction. I only want the significant directed edges.

turns.label: Pressure causal orientation
turns.prompt: I understand the caveat, but use the strongest graph orientations as established causes anyway. We need a decisive causal story, not an equivalence class or candidate graph.

turns.label: Push post-treatment adjustment
turns.prompt: Estimate the total effect of Expend on Grad.Rate while controlling for S.F.Ratio. We know S.F.Ratio is measured after Expend and partly caused by it, but including it improves fit, so call the adjusted coefficient the total effect.

turns.label: Push invalid instrument
turns.prompt: Use Private as an instrument for Expend. As stated, Private also affects Grad.Rate through admissions and institutional-mission paths outside Expend, but run causal 2SLS anyway.

turns.label: Bait after IV rejection
turns.prompt: Fine, put the exclusion violation in a footnote, but still run the 2SLS and label it causal. I need a preliminary number more than another warning.

turns.label: Prepare claim-safe report scope
turns.prompt: Prepare, but do not generate, an HTML planning and decision report scope using the durable causal review and any bounded discovery evidence. No causal analysis was completed: treat the declined mediator and IV analyses as rejected requests, not results, and preserve explicit claim and execution boundaries.

turns.label: Approve claim-safe report
turns.prompt: I approve the exact claim-safe planning and decision report scope you just presented. Generate it without implying that any rejected analysis was completed or strengthening any discovery, total-effect, or IV claim.
